sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing ankle swelling that was difficult to resolve even with bed elevation and difficulty breathing, necessitating propped-up positioning. He described a recent ambulance ride and ER visit due to these symptoms. He also reports almost incontinence and has been sleeping well. He denies chest pain.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure was recorded at 128/72 mmHg. Temperature was 98.7°F. Heart rate was 72 bpm. Respirations were 18 breaths per minute. Oxygen saturation was 96%. Examination revealed trace edema in the lower extremities. A two over six systolic ejection murmur was appreciated. The echocardiogram showed a preserved ejection fraction of 55% and abnormal diastolic filling with mild-to-moderate mitral regurgitation.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms and examination findings are consistent with congestive heart failure, likely exacerbated by dietary indiscretion and uncontrolled hypertension. The echocardiogram reveals evidence of diastolic dysfunction and mitral regurgitation.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Continue on bumex 2 mg daily. Monitor weight daily and report any gain of more than 3 pounds in 2 days. Order a renal artery ultrasound. Refer to a nutritionist for dietary counseling. Continue on cozaar 100 mg daily and norvasc 5 mg daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in three months.
